message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker after batch commit session

Update bead states to reflect completion of interactive refactor,
message render cache, CI correlation ID, and provider test commits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u1j1","title":"Task: Cass embedder selection wired to bake-off winner","description":"## Task
Wire cass embedding backend to the **winner selected in xf bake-off**.

## Blocked On
- xf bake-off report (bd-5fj3)

## Implementation Outline
1. Extend embedder registry to include winner backend
2. Add config entry (default_model + dimensions)
3. Add validation for model availability

## Validation & Tests
### Unit tests
- Registry selects winner backend
- dimensions() matches embedding length

### Integration tests
- Embed fixed corpus with winner model
- Compare against baseline metrics

### E2E script (detailed logging)
- `scripts/bakeoff/cass_embedder_e2e.sh`
  - Logs load time, RSS, latency

## Acceptance Criteria
- Cass loads winner model locally
- Model selection works via config + CLI
- No regression in lexical search
- Logs archived","notes":"### Optimization review
- If bake-off yields **no eligible** winners, allow baseline-only selection with explicit config flag and warning banner (mirrors xf report exception flag).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:22:45.765115415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T17:28:24.964946067Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T17:28:24.964588483Z","close_reason":"Embedder registry implemented: model selection via --model flag, validation, helpful errors. 11 unit tests + 10 E2E tests passing.","external_ref":"bd-2mbe","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u1j1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3c1ix","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:43:49Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":514,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u1j1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete. Created embedder_registry.rs with:
- EmbedderRegistry struct for model discovery/validation
- RegisteredEmbedder with name, id, dimension, description, availability checks
- EMBEDDERS static array (minilm, hash)
- get_embedder() and validate() functions
- Wired registry into lib.rs search code (semantic mode)
- 11 unit tests passing
- E2E script at scripts/bakeoff/cass_embedder_e2e.sh (10/10 tests pass)
- Invalid model name now shows helpful error with available models list","created_at":"2026-01-25T17:28:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u71u","title":"A6.3: Alternating color stripes for results","description":"Add alternating background colors to search results for better visual separation.","notes":"**Goal**: Zebra-striping like spreadsheets - alternating subtle background colors.

**Implementation in result rendering** (tui.rs ~1772):
```rust
for (idx, hit) in pane.hits.iter().enumerate() {
    let is_odd = idx % 2 == 1;
    let stripe_bg = if is_odd { palette.stripe_odd } else { palette.stripe_even };
    
    // Apply stripe_bg to all lines in this result
    let base_style = Style::default().bg(stripe_bg);
    // ... build lines with base_style ...
}
```

**Add to ThemePalette**:
```rust
pub stripe_even: Color,  // e.g., palette.bg (normal)
pub stripe_odd: Color,   // e.g., slightly lighter/darker
```

**Stripe colors**:
- Dark theme: even=#1a1b26, odd=#1e2030
- Light theme: even=#f0f0f5, odd=#e8e8f0

**Files to modify**:
- src/ui/components/theme.rs: add stripe colors
- src/ui/tui.rs: apply alternating bg in result list

**Testing**:
- Verify alternating colors visible
- Test with selected item (selection should override stripe)
- Test both themes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-28T22:00:00Z","updated_at":"2025-11-29T22:12:59.696429368Z","closed_at":"2025-11-29T22:12:59.696429368Z","external_ref":"bd-51f8","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u71u","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-sux.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:43:49Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1upf8","title":"Content Truncation (--max-content-length)","description":"# Content Truncation (--max-content-length)

## Problem Statement
The `content` and `snippet` fields can contain very long text (10KB+ for detailed conversations). Even when an agent wants content, they often only need the first few hundred characters to understand context.

## Proposed Solution
Add `--max-content-length N` flag to truncate text fields:
```bash
cass search "error" --json --max-content-length 200
```

Output:
```json
{"content": "First 200 chars of content...", "content_truncated": true, ...}
```

## Design Decisions

### Which Fields Are Affected
- `content` - main message content
- `snippet` - highlighted excerpt
- `title` - usually short, but truncate if needed

### Truncation Indicator
Add `_truncated` suffix field when content is truncated:
```json
{"content": "truncated...", "content_truncated": true}
```

### UTF-8 Safety
Truncate at character boundaries, not byte boundaries. Ensure valid UTF-8 output.

### Ellipsis
Append `...` when truncating to indicate incompleteness.

## Implementation
```rust
fn truncate_content(s: &str, max_len: usize) -> (String, bool) {
    if s.chars().count() <= max_len {
        (s.to_string(), false)
    } else {
        let truncated: String = s.chars().take(max_len.saturating_sub(3)).collect();
        (format!("{}...", truncated), true)
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--max-content-length 100` truncates content to ~100 chars
- [ ] Truncated fields have `_truncated: true` sibling field
- [ ] UTF-8 boundaries respected (no broken characters)
- [ ] Works with `--fields` (truncation applied to selected fields)
- [ ] Ellipsis appended to truncated content
- [ ] Test: various Unicode content truncation

## Effort Estimate
Low - 1-2 hours. Simple string truncation with UTF-8 awareness.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T23:54:08.438234185Z","updated_at":"2025-12-01T01:45:55.590679447Z","closed_at":"2025-12-01T01:45:55.590679447Z","external_ref":"bd-8e0e","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
